file.content:
"""PRD tracker reconciliation manifest (Wave 5 / WP-001 / WP-A02).

Reconciles PRD requirements against the workpackage tracker so wave-5 closeout
can prove every PRD acceptance axis has a tracker home (or is explicitly deferred).
Surfaces orphan PRD requirements and stale tracker rows in a single deterministic
manifest.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import hashlib
import json
import re
from dataclasses import dataclass
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Any

_REPO_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[2]
_PRD_PATH = _REPO_ROOT / "spec" / "prd" / "revit-production-parity-ai-agent-prd.md"
_TRACKER_PATH = _REPO_ROOT / "spec" / "revit-production-parity-workpackage-tracker.md"

COVERAGE_TOKENS: frozenset[str] = frozenset({"covered", "partial", "deferred", "orphan"})


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PrdAnchor:
    sectionId: str
    title: str
    level: int  # 2 for ##, 3 for ###


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TrackerRow:
    id: str
    title: str
    state: str


def _slugify(text: str) -> str:
    """Convert heading text to a stable snake_case section ID."""
    text = text.strip().lower()
    text = re.sub(r"[^a-z0-9]+", "_", text)
    return text.strip("_")


def parse_prd_section_anchors_v1(prd_path: str | Path) -> list[PrdAnchor]:
    """Parse ## and ### headings from the PRD into sorted PrdAnchor list."""
    text = Path(prd_path).read_text(encoding="utf-8")
    anchors: list[PrdAnchor] = []
    for line in text.splitlines():
        if line.startswith("### "):
            title = line[4:].strip()
            anchors.append(PrdAnchor(sectionId=_slugify(title), title=title, level=3))
        elif line.startswith("## "):
            title = line[3:].strip()
            anchors.append(PrdAnchor(sectionId=_slugify(title), title=title, level=2))
    anchors.sort(key=lambda a: a.sectionId)
    return anchors


def parse_tracker_workpackages_v1(tracker_path: str | Path) -> list[TrackerRow]:
    """Parse the ## Current Workpackages table into ordered TrackerRow list."""
    text = Path(tracker_path).read_text(encoding="utf-8")
    rows: list[TrackerRow] = []
    in_section = False
    for line in text.splitlines():
        if line.strip() == "## Current Workpackages":
            in_section = True
            continue
        if in_section and line.startswith("## "):
            break
        if in_section and line.startswith("| WP-"):
            parts = [p.strip() for p in line.split("|")]
            if len(parts) >= 4:
                rows.append(TrackerRow(id=parts[1], title=parts[2], state=parts[3]))
    return rows


# Hard-coded mapping: PRD sectionId → list of workpackage IDs (or "deferred" sentinel).
# This dict is the artifact — a hand-curated reconciliation of PRD sections to tracker WPs.
_PRD_TO_WORKPACKAGES: dict[str, list[str]] = {
    # §5 Screenshot-by-Screenshot Requirements
    "5_screenshot_by_screenshot_requirements": ["WP-A01", "WP-A02", "WP-A03"],
    "5_1_r1_exterior_3d_house_with_site_context": ["WP-B04", "WP-E01", "WP-E02", "WP-E03"],
    "5_2_r2_eg_floor_plan_with_doors_windows_stair_garage": [
        "WP-B02",
        "WP-B05",
        "WP-B06",
        "WP-C01",
        "WP-C02",
        "WP-C03",
    ],
    "5_3_r3_og_colored_room_plan": ["WP-B06", "WP-C04"],
    "5_4_r4_window_schedule": ["WP-D01", "WP-D02", "WP-D03", "WP-D04"],
    "5_5_r5_3d_cutaway_interior_view": ["WP-E01", "WP-E02", "WP-E03"],
    "5_6_r6_r7_sheet_layout_with_sections_details_titleblock": [
        "WP-E04",
        "WP-E05",
        "WP-E06",
    ],
    # §6 Model Kernel Requirements
    "6_model_kernel_requirements": [
        "WP-B01",
        "WP-B02",
        "WP-B03",
        "WP-B04",
        "WP-B05",
        "WP-B06",
    ],
    "6_1_semantic_source_of_truth": ["WP-000", "WP-X01"],
    "6_2_regeneration_engine": ["WP-B01", "WP-B02", "WP-B03", "WP-B04", "WP-B05"],
    # §7 Browser Product Requirements
    "7_browser_product_requirements": ["WP-C01", "WP-C05"],
    "7_1_workspace_layout": ["WP-C05"],
    "7_2_plan_authoring": ["WP-C01", "WP-C02", "WP-C03"],
    "7_3_documentation_view_rendering": ["WP-C01", "WP-C02", "WP-C03", "WP-E04"],
    # §8 AI-Agent Planning Requirements
    "8_ai_agent_planning_requirements": ["WP-F01", "WP-F02", "WP-F03"],
    "8_1_agent_inputs": ["WP-A01", "WP-F01"],
    "8_2_agent_planning_pipeline": ["WP-F01", "WP-F02"],
    "8_3_agent_evidence_artifacts": ["WP-A02", "WP-A03", "WP-F02", "WP-F03"],
    # §9 Schedules, Quantities, and Computations
    "9_schedules_quantities_and_computations": ["WP-D01", "WP-D02", "WP-D03"],
    # §10 Asset and Library Requirements
    "10_asset_and_library_requirements": ["WP-D04", "WP-D05", "WP-D06"],
    "10_1_family_type_libraries": ["WP-D04"],
    "10_2_asset_metadata": ["WP-D05", "WP-D06"],
    # §11 Validation and Advisor Requirements
    "11_validation_and_advisor_requirements": ["WP-A04", "WP-V01"],
    # §12 Exchange Requirements
    "12_exchange_requirements": [
        "WP-X01",
        "WP-X02",
        "WP-X03",
        "WP-X04",
        "WP-X05",
        "WP-X06",
    ],
    # §13 Performance and Collaboration
    "13_performance_and_collaboration": ["WP-P01", "WP-P02"],
    # §14 Roadmap
    "14_roadmap": ["WP-001"],
    "phase_a_evidence_and_baseline": ["WP-A01", "WP-A02", "WP-A03", "WP-A04"],
    "phase_b_real_residential_model_kernel": [
        "WP-B01",
        "WP-B02",
        "WP-B03",
        "WP-B04",
        "WP-B05",
        "WP-B06",
    ],
    "phase_c_production_plan_views": [
        "WP-C01",
        "WP-C02",
        "WP-C03",
        "WP-C04",
        "WP-C05",
    ],
    "phase_d_families_types_materials": [
        "WP-D01",
        "WP-D02",
        "WP-D03",
        "WP-D04",
        "WP-D05",
        "WP-D06",
    ],
    "phase_e_sections_3d_cutaways_sheets": [
        "WP-E01",
        "WP-E02",
        "WP-E03",
        "WP-E04",
        "WP-E05",
        "WP-E06",
    ],
    "phase_f_ai_agent_production_loop": ["WP-F01", "WP-F02", "WP-F03"],
    # §15 Verification Strategy
    "15_verification_strategy": ["WP-001", "WP-A01", "WP-A02", "WP-A03", "WP-A04"],
}

# Sections explicitly deferred (no workpackage assignment expected in v1 scope).
_DEFERRED_SECTIONS: frozenset[str] = frozenset(
    {
        "1_executive_summary",
        "2_reference_inputs",
        "2_1_supplied_revit_tutorial_screenshots",
        "2_2_existing_bim_ai_docs",
        "2_3_current_browser_baseline",
        "3_product_vision",
        "4_current_state_vs_target_state",
        "16_non_goals_and_guardrails",
        "17_open_questions",
        "18_immediate_recommendation",
    }
)


def _compute_coverage(
    section_id: str,
    wp_ids: list[str],
    state_by_wp: dict[str, str],
) -> str:
    """Derive the coverage token for one PRD anchor row."""
    if section_id in _DEFERRED_SECTIONS:
        return "deferred"
    if not wp_ids:
        return "orphan"
    if all(state_by_wp.get(wp_id, "partial") == "done" for wp_id in wp_ids):
        return "covered"
    return "partial"


def build_prd_tracker_reconciliation_manifest_v1(
    prd_path: str | Path | None = None,
    tracker_path: str | Path | None = None,
) -> dict[str, Any]:
    """Build the deterministic PRD-tracker reconciliation manifest.

    Returns a dict with prdTrackerReconciliationManifest_v1 token, schemaVersion,
    reconciliation rows sorted by sectionId, staleTrackerRows, and a SHA-256 digest.
    """
    prd_path = Path(prd_path) if prd_path else _PRD_PATH
    tracker_path = Path(tracker_path) if tracker_path else _TRACKER_PATH

    anchors = parse_prd_section_anchors_v1(prd_path)
    tracker_rows = parse_tracker_workpackages_v1(tracker_path)

    state_by_wp: dict[str, str] = {row.id: row.state for row in tracker_rows}

    # Build set of all WP IDs referenced in the mapping
    all_mapped_wp_ids: set[str] = set()
    for wp_list in _PRD_TO_WORKPACKAGES.values():
        all_mapped_wp_ids.update(wp_list)

    # Reconciliation rows — one per PRD anchor
    recon_rows: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
    for anchor in anchors:
        sid = anchor.sectionId
        wp_ids = sorted(_PRD_TO_WORKPACKAGES.get(sid, []))
        coverage = _compute_coverage(sid, wp_ids, state_by_wp)
        recon_rows.append(
            {
                "prdSectionId": sid,
                "prdSectionTitle": anchor.title,
                "prdSectionLevel": anchor.level,
                "workpackageIds": wp_ids,
                "coverage": coverage,
            }
        )

    recon_rows.sort(key=lambda r: str(r["prdSectionId"]))

    # Stale tracker rows — tracker WP IDs not referenced in the mapping at all
    stale_rows: list[dict[str, Any]] = [
        {"workpackageId": row.id, "title": row.title, "state": row.state}
        for row in tracker_rows
        if row.id not in all_mapped_wp_ids
    ]
    stale_rows.sort(key=lambda r: str(r["workpackageId"]))

    # Coverage summary counts
    coverage_counts: dict[str, int] = {t: 0 for t in sorted(COVERAGE_TOKENS)}
    for row in recon_rows:
        t = str(row.get("coverage") or "")
        if t in coverage_counts:
            coverage_counts[t] += 1

    body: dict[str, Any] = {
        "format": "prdTrackerReconciliationManifest_v1",
        "schemaVersion": 1,
        "reconciliationRows": recon_rows,
        "staleTrackerRows": stale_rows,
        "coverageCounts": coverage_counts,
        "allowedCoverageTokens": sorted(COVERAGE_TOKENS),
        "note": (
            "Deterministic PRD-tracker reconciliation for WP-001/WP-A02 wave-5 closeout. "
            "Mapping is hand-curated — the dict is itself the artifact. "
            "orphan rows have no tracker assignment and are not explicitly deferred."
        ),
    }

    canonical = json.dumps(body, sort_keys=True, separators=(",", ":"))
    digest = hashlib.sha256(canonical.encode("utf-8")).hexdigest()
    return {**body, "prdTrackerReconciliationDigestSha256": digest}

"""PRD closeout cross-correlation manifest (Wave 3 / WP-A01/A02/A04/F01/V01).

Cross-correlates PRD §11–§15 advisor matrix sections with v1 closeout readiness
manifest gates and prd traceability test rows. Produces a deterministic manifest
with one row per PRD section and a crossCorrelationToken per row.

Surfaces:
  - prdAdvisorMatrix_v1 (advisor matrix status per section)
  - v1CloseoutReadinessManifest_v1 (CI gates)
  - prd_traceability_matrix test rows

Token vocabulary (in priority order):
  status_drift       — advisor section has block status but no readiness gate is failing
  reason_code_drift  — deferred section waiver code not explicitly mirrored in readiness
  advisor_only       — section in advisor matrix only; no specific readiness gate or traceability
  aligned            — section has specific readiness gate AND traceability test coverage
  readiness_only     — gate in readiness manifest with no advisor matrix section (rare)

Note: this module intentionally does NOT import v1_closeout_readiness_manifest to avoid
a circular dependency (the readiness manifest embeds this manifest). Gate structural status
is computed directly from file-system checks (same logic as the readiness manifest gates).
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import hashlib
import json
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Any

    {
        "aligned",
        "advisor_only",
        "readiness_only",
        "status_drift",
        "reason_code_drift",
    }

def _canonical_without_digest(manifest: dict) -> str:
toolUseResult.originalFile:
from __future__ import annotations

import hashlib
import json
from pathlib import Path

import pytest

from bim_ai import v1_closeout_readiness_manifest as v1_manifest
from bim_ai.evidence_manifest import agent_evidence_closure_hints
from bim_ai.v1_closeout_readiness_manifest import build_v1_closeout_readiness_manifest_v1

REPO_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent.parent

_REQUIRED_WP = ("WP-A01", "WP-A02", "WP-A04", "WP-V01", "WP-F01")


def _canonical_without_digest(manifest: dict) -> str:
    body = {k: v for k, v in manifest.items() if k != "manifestContentDigestSha256"}
    return json.dumps(body, sort_keys=True, separators=(",", ":"))


def test_closeout_manifest_is_deterministic_across_calls() -> None:
    a = build_v1_closeout_readiness_manifest_v1()
    b = build_v1_closeout_readiness_manifest_v1()
    assert a == b
    dig_a = a.get("manifestContentDigestSha256")
    dig_b = b.get("manifestContentDigestSha256")
    assert isinstance(dig_a, str) and len(dig_a) == 64
    assert dig_a == dig_b


def test_manifest_digest_matches_canonical_body() -> None:
    m = build_v1_closeout_readiness_manifest_v1()
    expected = hashlib.sha256(_canonical_without_digest(m).encode("utf-8")).hexdigest()
    assert m.get("manifestContentDigestSha256") == expected


def test_required_gate_paths_and_kinds() -> None:
    m = build_v1_closeout_readiness_manifest_v1()
    assert m.get("format") == "v1CloseoutReadinessManifest_v1"
    gates = m.get("gates")
    assert isinstance(gates, list)
    by_id = {str(g["id"]): g for g in gates if isinstance(g, dict)}
    assert (
        by_id["pytest_prd_traceability_matrix"]["path"]
        == "app/tests/test_prd_traceability_matrix.py"
    )
    assert (
        by_id["pytest_one_family_bundle_roundtrip"]["path"]
        == "app/tests/test_one_family_bundle_roundtrip.py"
    )
    assert (
        by_id["pytest_evidence_manifest_closure"]["path"]
        == "app/tests/test_evidence_manifest_closure.py"
    )
    assert by_id["golden_cli_one_family_commands"]["gateKind"] == "golden_cli_bundle_anchor"
    assert by_id["pytest_one_family_bundle_roundtrip"]["gateKind"] == "golden_bundle_roundtrip"
    gate_ids = [str(g["id"]) for g in gates if isinstance(g, dict)]
    assert gate_ids == sorted(gate_ids)


@pytest.mark.skipif(
    not (REPO_ROOT / "spec" / "revit-production-parity-workpackage-tracker.md").exists(),
    reason="spec/revit-production-parity-workpackage-tracker.md was deleted (superseded by workpackage-master-tracker.md)",
)
def test_tracker_contains_required_workpackages() -> None:
    tr = REPO_ROOT / "spec" / "revit-production-parity-workpackage-tracker.md"
    text = tr.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
    for wp in _REQUIRED_WP:
        assert f"| {wp} |" in text


def test_golden_cli_gate_structural_ok_in_repo() -> None:
    m = build_v1_closeout_readiness_manifest_v1()
    gates = {g["id"]: g for g in m["gates"] if isinstance(g, dict)}
    g = gates["golden_cli_one_family_commands"]
    assert g["structuralOk"] is True
    p = REPO_ROOT / g["path"]
    assert p.is_file()


def test_deferred_blockers_not_completed_work() -> None:
    m = build_v1_closeout_readiness_manifest_v1()
    for row in m["deferredBlockers"]:
        assert row.get("countsAsCompletedWork") is False
    ids = [str(r["id"]) for r in m["deferredBlockers"]]
    assert "prd_wide_blocking_validation" in ids
    assert sorted(ids) == ids


def test_default_classification_blocked_by_deferrals_never_release_ready() -> None:
    m = build_v1_closeout_readiness_manifest_v1()
    assert m["releaseClassification"] == "blocked_deferred"
    assert m["releaseClassification"] != "release_ready"


def test_classification_not_release_ready_when_deferrals_cleared(
    monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch,
) -> None:
    monkeypatch.setattr(v1_manifest, "_deferred_blockers_v1", lambda: [])
    m = build_v1_closeout_readiness_manifest_v1()
    assert m["releaseClassification"] == "not_release_ready"
    assert m["releaseClassification"] != "release_ready"


def test_classification_not_release_ready_when_gate_fails(monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch) -> None:
    monkeypatch.setattr(v1_manifest, "_deferred_blockers_v1", lambda: [])

    def bad_gates(
        *,
        tracker_text: str | None = None,
        ci_yml_text: str | None = None,
    ) -> list[dict]:
        return [
            {
                "id": "pytest_evidence_manifest_closure",
                "gateKind": "required_pytest_module",
                "path": "app/tests/test_evidence_manifest_closure.py",
                "structuralOk": False,
            }
        ]

    monkeypatch.setattr(v1_manifest, "_gate_rows", bad_gates)
    m = build_v1_closeout_readiness_manifest_v1()
    assert m["releaseClassification"] == "not_release_ready"
    details = m.get("releaseClassificationDetails")
    assert isinstance(details, dict)
    assert details.get("allRequiredGatesStructuralOk") is False


def test_agent_evidence_closure_hints_names_manifest_field() -> None:
    hints = agent_evidence_closure_hints()
    assert hints.get("v1CloseoutReadinessManifestField") == "v1CloseoutReadinessManifest_v1"
    note = str(hints.get("semanticDigestOmitsDerivativeSummariesNote") or "")
    assert "v1CloseoutReadinessManifest_v1" in note


def test_agent_evidence_closure_hints_names_prd_advisor_matrix_field() -> None:
    hints = agent_evidence_closure_hints()
    assert hints.get("prdAdvisorMatrixField") == "prdAdvisorMatrix_v1"
    note = str(hints.get("semanticDigestOmitsDerivativeSummariesNote") or "")
    assert "prdAdvisorMatrix_v1" in note


def test_manifest_includes_prd_advisor_matrix_summary() -> None:
    m = build_v1_closeout_readiness_manifest_v1()
    summary = m.get("prdAdvisorMatrixSummary")
    assert isinstance(summary, dict), "manifest must include prdAdvisorMatrixSummary"
    assert summary.get("format") == "prdAdvisorMatrixSummary_v1"
    assert isinstance(summary.get("totalSections"), int) and summary["totalSections"] > 0
    assert "statusCounts" in summary
    assert "deferredCount" in summary
    assert "passCount" in summary


def test_manifest_prd_advisor_matrix_summary_deferred_count_positive() -> None:
    m = build_v1_closeout_readiness_manifest_v1()
    summary = m["prdAdvisorMatrixSummary"]
    assert summary["deferredCount"] > 0, (
        "At least one PRD section must be deferred in the closeout manifest summary"
    )


def test_manifest_gates_include_prd_blocking_advisor_matrix() -> None:
    m = build_v1_closeout_readiness_manifest_v1()
    gates = {g["id"]: g for g in m.get("gates", []) if isinstance(g, dict)}
    assert "pytest_prd_blocking_advisor_matrix" in gates
    gate = gates["pytest_prd_blocking_advisor_matrix"]
    assert gate["path"] == "app/tests/test_prd_blocking_advisor_matrix.py"
    assert gate["gateKind"] == "required_pytest_module"
    assert gate["structuralOk"] is True

    assert g["structuralOk"] is True
    p = REPO_ROOT / g["path"]
    assert p.is_file()


def test_deferred_blockers_not_completed_work() -> None:
    m = build_v1_closeout_readiness_manifest_v1()
    for row in m["deferredBlockers"]:
        assert row.get("countsAsCompletedWork") is False
    ids = [str(r["id"]) for r in m["deferredBlockers"]]
    assert "prd_wide_blocking_validation" in ids
    assert sorted(ids) == ids


def test_default_classification_blocked_by_deferrals_never_release_ready() -> None:
    m = build_v1_closeout_readiness_manifest_v1()
    assert m["releaseClassification"] == "blocked_deferred"
    assert m["releaseClassification"] != "release_ready"


def test_classification_not_release_ready_when_deferrals_cleared(
    monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch,
) -> None:
    monkeypatch.setattr(v1_manifest, "_deferred_blockers_v1", lambda: [])
    m = build_v1_closeout_readiness_manifest_v1()
    assert m["releaseClassification"] == "not_release_ready"
    assert m["releaseClassification"] != "release_ready"


def test_classification_not_release_ready_when_gate_fails(monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch) -> None:
    monkeypatch.setattr(v1_manifest, "_deferred_blockers_v1", lambda: [])

    def bad_gates(
        *,
        tracker_text: str | None = None,
        ci_yml_text: str | None = None,
    ) -> list[dict]:
        return [
            {
                "id": "pytest_evidence_manifest_closure",
                "gateKind": "required_pytest_module",
                "path": "app/tests/test_evidence_manifest_closure.py",
                "structuralOk": False,
            }
        ]

    monkeypatch.setattr(v1_manifest, "_gate_rows", bad_gates)
    m = build_v1_closeout_readiness_manifest_v1()
    assert m["releaseClassification"] == "not_release_ready"
    details = m.get("releaseClassificationDetails")
    assert isinstance(details, dict)
    assert details.get("allRequiredGatesStructuralOk") is False


def test_agent_evidence_closure_hints_names_manifest_field() -> None:
    hints = agent_evidence_closure_hints()
    assert hints.get("v1CloseoutReadinessManifestField") == "v1CloseoutReadinessManifest_v1"
    note = str(hints.get("semanticDigestOmitsDerivativeSummariesNote") or "")
    assert "v1CloseoutReadinessManifest_v1" in note


def test_agent_evidence_closure_hints_names_prd_advisor_matrix_field() -> None:
    hints = agent_evidence_closure_hints()
    assert hints.get("prdAdvisorMatrixField") == "prdAdvisorMatrix_v1"
    note = str(hints.get("semanticDigestOmitsDerivativeSummariesNote") or "")
    assert "prdAdvisorMatrix_v1" in note


def test_manifest_includes_prd_advisor_matrix_summary() -> None:
    m = build_v1_closeout_readiness_manifest_v1()
    summary = m.get("prdAdvisorMatrixSummary")
    assert isinstance(summary, dict), "manifest must include prdAdvisorMatrixSummary"
    assert summary.get("format") == "prdAdvisorMatrixSummary_v1"
    assert isinstance(summary.get("totalSections"), int) and summary["totalSections"] > 0
    assert "statusCounts" in summary
    assert "deferredCount" in summary
    assert "passCount" in summary


def test_manifest_prd_advisor_matrix_summary_deferred_count_positive() -> None:
    m = build_v1_closeout_readiness_manifest_v1()
    summary = m["prdAdvisorMatrixSummary"]
    assert summary["deferredCount"] > 0, (
        "At least one PRD section must be deferred in the closeout manifest summary"
    )


def test_manifest_gates_include_prd_blocking_advisor_matrix() -> None:
    m = build_v1_closeout_readiness_manifest_v1()
    gates = {g["id"]: g for g in m.get("gates", []) if isinstance(g, dict)}
    assert "pytest_prd_blocking_advisor_matrix" in gates
    gate = gates["pytest_prd_blocking_advisor_matrix"]
    assert gate["path"] == "app/tests/test_prd_blocking_advisor_matrix.py"
    assert gate["gateKind"] == "required_pytest_module"
    assert gate["structuralOk"] is True

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# BIM AI — Workpackage Tracker

Single source of truth for outstanding implementation work. Waves 0–6 are merged to main; their prompt files have been deleted per protocol. This tracker now lists only the **post-Wave-6 backlog** consolidated from former specs (`rendering-v2-spec.md`, `prd/cli-agent-home-design-gaps.md`, `target-house-seed.md` §3, `ifc-export-wp-x03-slice.md` "Still deferred", PRD §14).

Verified against code on 2026-05-06; everything not listed here is shipped.

## Status Legend

| Symbol     | Meaning                                                         |
| ---------- | --------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `done`     | Meets the done rule — tested, type-clean, merged to main        |
| `partial`  | Some slice exists; measurable progress; spec requirements unmet |
| `open`     | Not started                                                     |
| `deferred` | Explicitly out of scope for current roadmap                     |

## Done Rule

A workpackage is `done` when all of: (a) `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit` clean; (b) new logic has vitest / pytest unit coverage; (c) `make verify` passes; (d) merged to main and pushed.

---

## Rendering / Family System

| ID     | Item                                                | Note                                                                    | State     |
| ------ | --------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------- |
| FL-06  | Family Library Browser Panel                        | `FamilyLibraryPanel.tsx` + `thumbnailCache.ts` in flight (uncommitted)  | `partial` |
| FL-08  | System family layer-stack renderer + plan lines     | `wallTypeCatalog.ts` + `meshBuilders.layeredWall.ts` in flight (uncommitted) | `partial` |
| GAP-R5 | Floor slab edge expression                          | Thin horizontal strip in `makeWallMesh` for upper-floor walls           | `open`    |
| GAP-R7 | Interior visible through curtain-wall glass         | Likely `depthWrite: false` on `glassMat` in `makeCurtainWallMesh`       | `open`    |

## Exchange Formats

| ID     | Item                                                | Note                                                                    | State  |
| ------ | --------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------ |
| IFC-01 | `roofTypeId` round-trip through IFC                 | Kernel has the field; `app/bim_ai/export_ifc.py` doesn't write it       | `open` |
| IFC-02 | Distinguish `gable_pitched_rectangle` in IFC body   | Currently always emits prism mass                                       | `open` |
| IFC-03 | Roof-hosted void replay                             | Today rolled up as `slabRoofHostedVoidReplaySkipped_v0` only            | `open` |
| IFC-04 | Broader QTO + materials + classifications + composites | Narrow QTO slice shipped; full takeoff pending                       | `open` |
| GLT-01 | Draco / mesh compression on glTF export             | Tracked previously under WP-X02                                         | `open` |

## CLI / Agent Loop

| ID     | Item                                                | Note                                                                    | State  |
| ------ | --------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------ |
| CLI-01 | `bim-ai export json`                                | Stub at `packages/cli/cli.mjs:404`; ifc / gltf / glb already shipped    | `open` |
| CLI-02 | `bim-ai diff --from … --to …`                       | Stub at `packages/cli/cli.mjs:372`                                      | `open` |
| AGT-01 | Closed iterative-correction agent loop              | `evidence-package` + `apply-bundle --dry-run` ship; loop wiring manual  | `open` |

## Kernel + Validation + Plan Views

| ID     | Item                                                | Note                                                                    | State  |
| ------ | --------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------ |
| KRN-01 | `property_line` element kind                        | `SiteElem.uniform_setback_mm` exists; no line element yet               | `open` |
| KRN-02 | Concave / L-shaped roof footprint 3D mesh           | Today: `valley_candidate_deferred` placeholder; section view OK         | `open` |
| KRN-03 | Hip roof (convex polygon >4 corners) 3D mesh        | Today: `hip_candidate_deferred` placeholder; section view OK            | `open` |
| VAL-01 | Topological room enclosure (wall-graph closure)     | Replaces the `room_no_door` centroid heuristic                          | `open` |
| PLN-01 | Dimension / tag automation                          | Manual placement works; auto-dimension / auto-tag missing               | `open` |

---

## Locked Decisions

These answers to PRD §17 govern implementation defaults. Revisit quarterly or when IFC scope expands.

| Topic                    | Decision                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       |
| ------------------------ | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Regional building code   | **EU residential proxy** — mm dimensions; staircase comfort `tread ≥ 260 mm × riser ≤ 190 mm` as advisory only; egress not enforced in v1.                                                                                                     |
| Residential vs cleanroom | **Residential reference house first**; cleanroom class / pressure / interlock metadata supported in schema; **IDS fixtures** phased after IFC subset.                                                                                          |
| Titleblock / sheet sizes | First-class layout: **A1 landscape metaphor** (`594×841` mm portrait stored as ×1000 coords in existing `sheet`/`viewportsMm` convention — keep mm paper space); titleblock strings: project name, sheet number, revision, drawn/checked/date. |
| AI vision automation     | **Assumptions-first** — agent MUST log assumptions JSON before apply; automated screenshot comparison is CI opt-in (`compare` PNG diff tolerances), not silent vision-from-screenshot in core product.                                         |

## OpenBIM Stance

Integration ladder (priority order): (1) JSON command bundles + snapshots → (2) IFC import/export → (3) BCF → (4) IDS → (5) glTF → (6) RVT bridge. **Native RVT I/O is explicitly deferred** until OpenBIM semantics stabilise; safest delivery would be plugin / Autodesk cloud conversion / customer-specific pipeline, not opaque-binary import.

Engineering invariants: (a) Web + CLI must stay symmetric — anything the UI edits must be reproducible via API commands logged in undo stacks; (b) authoring-side IDS checks live in `constraints.evaluate`; exported IFC semantics summarised by `inspect_kernel_ifc_semantics()` / `summarize_kernel_ifc_semantic_roundtrip()` in `app/bim_ai/export_ifc.py`.

---

## Reference Files

`spec/` only contains:

- `workpackage-master-tracker.md` — **this file, single source of truth**.
- `target-house-seed.md` — architect-quality description of the demo seed house (kept as reference data, not backlog).
- `prd/revit-production-parity-ai-agent-prd.md` — load-bearing requirements anchor; `app/tests/test_prd_traceability_matrix.py`, `app/tests/test_prd_blocking_advisor_matrix.py`, and the runtime API at `app/bim_ai/{prd_blocking_advisor_matrix,prd_tracker_reconciliation_v1,v1_closeout_readiness_manifest}.py` assert verbatim "needle strings" exist in it. Treat it as a sealed test fixture — do not edit unless you also update those modules.

The actual product behaviour is the source of truth for everything else: code (`app/`, `packages/`), CLI `--help`, API schemas, and tests.

## Update Protocol

1. When a backlog item ships: change `open` / `partial` → `done` in the relevant table.
2. Once a category is fully drained (all rows `done`), drop the table.
3. New work starts as a row here; only spin up a fresh wave / prompt directory if a coordinated multi-WP batch is needed.

content.text:
Done. `spec/` is now down to **2 markdown files**.

```
spec/
├── workpackage-master-tracker.md   ← single tracker (~95 lines)
├── target-house-seed.md            ← architectural ground truth
└── examples/, *.png assets
```

**Refactor summary:**

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `app/bim_ai/prd_blocking_advisor_matrix.py` | Updated header comment; updated 3 deferred-row `waiverEvidenceLink` strings to point at the new tracker |
| `app/bim_ai/v1_closeout_readiness_manifest.py` | Removed `_PRD_PATH`, `_TRACKER_PATH`, `_REQUIRED_WP_IDS`; dropped `prd_file_present` and `tracker_workpackages_present` gates (the latter was already silently broken); cleaned `agentNextActions`; simplified `_gate_rows` signature |
| `app/bim_ai/prd_tracker_reconciliation_v1.py` | **Deleted** — was only consumed by its own already-skipped test |
| `app/tests/test_prd_blocking_advisor_matrix.py` | Removed `test_prd_needles_exist_in_prd_source`; removed `PRD_PATH` constant; updated docstring + waiver fixture strings |
| `app/tests/test_prd_traceability_matrix.py` | Rewrote to drop PRD-anchor assertion; kept structural path-existence assertion. Row IDs (`pytest_prd_traceability_matrix`) preserved so the cross-correlation manifest stays consistent |
| `app/tests/test_prd_tracker_reconciliation_v1.py` | **Deleted** alongside its module |
| `app/tests/test_v1_closeout_readiness_manifest.py` | Removed already-skipped `test_tracker_contains_required_workpackages`; updated `bad_gates` mock signature |
| `spec/prd/revit-production-parity-ai-agent-prd.md` | **Deleted** (740 lines) |
| `spec/prd/` directory | Removed (now empty) |
| `spec/workpackage-master-tracker.md` | Tightened lead-in + reference-files note |

**Test verification:**
- 89 PRD-related tests pass (`test_prd_traceability_matrix`, `test_prd_blocking_advisor_matrix`, `test_v1_closeout_readiness_manifest`, `test_prd_closeout_cross_correlation`, `test_advisor_blocking_class_expansion`, `test_final_hardening_sweep`)
- 42 downstream tests pass (`test_evidence_manifest_closure`, `test_one_family_bundle_roundtrip`)
- Zero remaining stale references to deleted files anywhere in `app/`, `packages/`, or `spec/`

**Runtime API behaviour:**
- `prdAdvisorMatrix_v1` still emitted via `evidence-package` endpoint — content unchanged (validation classes still documented as `prdNeedle` field values, just no longer required to exist in an external markdown file)
- `v1CloseoutReadinessManifest_v1` shape unchanged except the obsolete `prd_file_present` and `tracker_workpackages_present` gate rows are gone (those were already misleading: one would soon be false; the other was silently false)
- `prdCloseoutCrossCorrelationManifest_v1` unchanged — it never read the PRD file

All staged in your worktree, nothing committed.

content.content:
1	# BIM AI — Workpackage Tracker
2	
3	Single source of truth for outstanding implementation work. Waves 0–6 are merged to main; their prompt files have been deleted per protocol. Earlier wave-by-wave specs and the screenshot-driven PRD have been consolidated here.
4	
5	Verified against code on 2026-05-07; everything not listed here is shipped.
6	
7	## Status Legend
8	
9	| Symbol     | Meaning                                                         |
10	| ---------- | --------------------------------------------------------------- |
11	| `done`     | Meets the done rule — tested, type-clean, merged to main        |
12	| `partial`  | Some slice exists; measurable progress; spec requirements unmet |
13	| `open`     | Not started                                                     |
14	| `deferred` | Explicitly out of scope for current roadmap                     |
15	
16	## Done Rule
17	
18	A workpackage is `done` when all of: (a) `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit` clean; (b) new logic has vitest / pytest unit coverage; (c) `make verify` passes; (d) merged to main and pushed.
19	
20	---
21	
22	## Rendering / Family System
23	
24	| ID     | Item                                                | Note                                                                    | State     |
25	| ------ | --------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------- |
26	| FL-06  | Family Library Browser Panel                        | `FamilyLibraryPanel.tsx` + `thumbnailCache.ts` in flight (uncommitted)  | `partial` |
27	| FL-08  | System family layer-stack renderer + plan lines     | `wallTypeCatalog.ts` + `meshBuilders.layeredWall.ts` in flight (uncommitted) | `partial` |
28	| GAP-R5 | Floor slab edge expression                          | Thin horizontal strip in `makeWallMesh` for upper-floor walls           | `open`    |
29	| GAP-R7 | Interior visible through curtain-wall glass         | Likely `depthWrite: false` on `glassMat` in `makeCurtainWallMesh`       | `open`    |
30	
31	## Exchange Formats
32	
33	| ID     | Item                                                | Note                                                                    | State  |
34	| ------ | --------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------ |
35	| IFC-01 | `roofTypeId` round-trip through IFC                 | Kernel has the field; `app/bim_ai/export_ifc.py` doesn't write it       | `open` |
36	| IFC-02 | Distinguish `gable_pitched_rectangle` in IFC body   | Currently always emits prism mass                                       | `open` |
37	| IFC-03 | Roof-hosted void replay                             | Today rolled up as `slabRoofHostedVoidReplaySkipped_v0` only            | `open` |
38	| IFC-04 | Broader QTO + materials + classifications + composites | Narrow QTO slice shipped; full takeoff pending                       | `open` |
39	| GLT-01 | Draco / mesh compression on glTF export             | Tracked previously under WP-X02                                         | `open` |
40	
41	## CLI / Agent Loop
42	
43	| ID     | Item                                                | Note                                                                    | State  |
44	| ------ | --------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------ |
45	| CLI-01 | `bim-ai export json`                                | Stub at `packages/cli/cli.mjs:404`; ifc / gltf / glb already shipped    | `open` |
46	| CLI-02 | `bim-ai diff --from … --to …`                       | Stub at `packages/cli/cli.mjs:372`                                      | `open` |
47	| AGT-01 | Closed iterative-correction agent loop              | `evidence-package` + `apply-bundle --dry-run` ship; loop wiring manual  | `open` |
48	
49	## Kernel + Validation + Plan Views
50	
51	| ID     | Item                                                | Note                                                                    | State  |
52	| ------ | --------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------ |
53	| KRN-01 | `property_line` element kind                        | `SiteElem.uniform_setback_mm` exists; no line element yet               | `open` |
54	| KRN-02 | Concave / L-shaped roof footprint 3D mesh           | Today: `valley_candidate_deferred` placeholder; section view OK         | `open` |
55	| KRN-03 | Hip roof (convex polygon >4 corners) 3D mesh        | Today: `hip_candidate_deferred` placeholder; section view OK            | `open` |
56	| VAL-01 | Topological room enclosure (wall-graph closure)     | Replaces the `room_no_door` centroid heuristic                          | `open` |
57	| PLN-01 | Dimension / tag automation                          | Manual placement works; auto-dimension / auto-tag missing               | `open` |
58	
59	---
60	
61	## Locked Decisions
62	
63	These answers to PRD §17 govern implementation defaults. Revisit quarterly or when IFC scope expands.
64	
65	| Topic                    | Decision                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       |
66	| ------------------------ | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
67	| Regional building code   | **EU residential proxy** — mm dimensions; staircase comfort `tread ≥ 260 mm × riser ≤ 190 mm` as advisory only; egress not enforced in v1.                                                                                                     |
68	| Residential vs cleanroom | **Residential reference house first**; cleanroom class / pressure / interlock metadata supported in schema; **IDS fixtures** phased after IFC subset.                                                                                          |
69	| Titleblock / sheet sizes | First-class layout: **A1 landscape metaphor** (`594×841` mm portrait stored as ×1000 coords in existing `sheet`/`viewportsMm` convention — keep mm paper space); titleblock strings: project name, sheet number, revision, drawn/checked/date. |
70	| AI vision automation     | **Assumptions-first** — agent MUST log assumptions JSON before apply; automated screenshot comparison is CI opt-in (`compare` PNG diff tolerances), not silent vision-from-screenshot in core product.                                         |
71	
72	## OpenBIM Stance
73	
74	Integration ladder (priority order): (1) JSON command bundles + snapshots → (2) IFC import/export → (3) BCF → (4) IDS → (5) glTF → (6) RVT bridge. **Native RVT I/O is explicitly deferred** until OpenBIM semantics stabilise; safest delivery would be plugin / Autodesk cloud conversion / customer-specific pipeline, not opaque-binary import.
75	
76	Engineering invariants: (a) Web + CLI must stay symmetric — anything the UI edits must be reproducible via API commands logged in undo stacks; (b) authoring-side IDS checks live in `constraints.evaluate`; exported IFC semantics summarised by `inspect_kernel_ifc_semantics()` / `summarize_kernel_ifc_semantic_roundtrip()` in `app/bim_ai/export_ifc.py`.
77	
78	---
79	
80	## Reference Files
81	
82	`spec/` only contains:
83	
84	- `workpackage-master-tracker.md` — **this file, single source of truth**.
85	- `target-house-seed.md` — architect-quality description of the demo seed house (kept as reference data, not backlog).
86	
87	The actual product behaviour is the source of truth for everything else: code (`app/`, `packages/`), CLI `--help`, API schemas, and tests. The validation taxonomy formerly anchored in a markdown PRD now lives in code at `app/bim_ai/prd_blocking_advisor_matrix.py` (each row's `prdNeedle` field).
88	
89	## Update Protocol
90	
91	1. When a backlog item ships: change `open` / `partial` → `done` in the relevant table.
92	2. Once a category is fully drained (all rows `done`), drop the table.
93	3. New work starts as a row here; only spin up a fresh wave / prompt directory if a coordinated multi-WP batch is needed.
94

## Done Rule

A workpackage is `done` when all of: (a) `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit` clean; (b) new logic has vitest / pytest unit coverage; (c) `make verify` passes; (d) merged to main and pushed.

---

## Exchange Formats

| IFC-02 | Distinguish `gable_pitched_rectangle` in IFC body   | Currently always emits prism mass                                       | `open` |
| IFC-03 | Roof-hosted void replay                             | Today rolled up as `slabRoofHostedVoidReplaySkipped_v0` only            | `open` |

## CLI / Agent Loop

| CLI-01 | `bim-ai export json`                                | Stub at `packages/cli/cli.mjs:404`; ifc / gltf / glb already shipped    | `open` |
| CLI-02 | `bim-ai diff --from … --to …`                       | Stub at `packages/cli/cli.mjs:372`                                      | `open` |
| AGT-01 | Closed iterative-correction agent loop              | `evidence-package` + `apply-bundle --dry-run` ship; loop wiring manual  | `open` |

## Kernel + Validation + Plan Views

| ID     | Item                                                | Note                                                                    | State  |
| ------ | --------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------ |
| KRN-01 | `property_line` element kind                        | `SiteElem.uniform_setback_mm` exists; no line element yet               | `open` |
| KRN-02 | Concave / L-shaped roof footprint 3D mesh           | Today: `valley_candidate_deferred` placeholder; section view OK         | `open` |
| KRN-03 | Hip roof (convex polygon >4 corners) 3D mesh        | Today: `hip_candidate_deferred` placeholder; section view OK            | `open` |
| VAL-01 | Topological room enclosure (wall-graph closure)     | Replaces the `room_no_door` centroid heuristic                          | `open` |
| PLN-01 | Dimension / tag automation                          | Manual placement works; auto-dimension / auto-tag missing               | `open` |

---

## Locked Decisions

These answers to PRD §17 govern implementation defaults. Revisit quarterly or when IFC scope expands.

| Topic                    | Decision                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       |
| ------------------------ | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Regional building code   | **EU residential proxy** — mm dimensions; staircase comfort `tread ≥ 260 mm × riser ≤ 190 mm` as advisory only; egress not enforced in v1.                                                                                                     |
| Residential vs cleanroom | **Residential reference house first**; cleanroom class / pressure / interlock metadata supported in schema; **IDS fixtures** phased after IFC subset.                                                                                          |
| Titleblock / sheet sizes | First-class layout: **A1 landscape metaphor** (`594×841` mm portrait stored as ×1000 coords in existing `sheet`/`viewportsMm` convention — keep mm paper space); titleblock strings: project name, sheet number, revision, drawn/checked/date. |
| AI vision automation     | **Assumptions-first** — agent MUST log assumptions JSON before apply; automated screenshot comparison is CI opt-in (`compare` PNG diff tolerances), not silent vision-from-screenshot in core product.                                         |

## OpenBIM Stance

Integration ladder (priority order): (1) JSON command bundles + snapshots → (2) IFC import/export → (3) BCF → (4) IDS → (5) glTF → (6) RVT bridge. **Native RVT I/O is explicitly deferred** until OpenBIM semantics stabilise; safest delivery would be plugin / Autodesk cloud conversion / customer-specific pipeline, not opaque-binary import.

Engineering invariants: (a) Web + CLI must stay symmetric — anything the UI edits must be reproducible via API commands logged in undo stacks; (b) authoring-side IDS checks live in `constraints.evaluate`; exported IFC semantics summarised by `inspect_kernel_ifc_semantics()` / `summarize_kernel_ifc_semantic_roundtrip()` in `app/bim_ai/export_ifc.py`.

---

## Reference Files

`spec/` only contains:

- `workpackage-master-tracker.md` — **this file, single source of truth**.
- `target-house-seed.md` — architect-quality description of the demo seed house (kept as reference data, not backlog).

The actual product behaviour is the source of truth for everything else: code (`app/`, `packages/`), CLI `--help`, API schemas, and tests. The validation taxonomy formerly anchored in a markdown PRD now lives in code at `app/bim_ai/prd_blocking_advisor_matrix.py` (each row's `prdNeedle` field).

## Update Protocol

1. When a backlog item ships: change `open` / `partial` → `done` in the relevant table.
2. Once a category is fully drained (all rows `done`), drop the table.
3. New work starts as a row here; only spin up a fresh wave / prompt directory if a coordinated multi-WP batch is needed.

# BIM AI — Workpackage Tracker

Single source of truth for outstanding implementation work. Waves 0–6 are merged to main; their prompt files have been deleted per protocol. Earlier wave-by-wave specs and the screenshot-driven PRD have been consolidated here.

Verified against code on 2026-05-07; everything not listed here is shipped.

## Status Legend

| Symbol     | Meaning                                                         |
| ---------- | --------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `done`     | Meets the done rule — tested, type-clean, merged to main        |
| `partial`  | Some slice exists; measurable progress; spec requirements unmet |
| `open`     | Not started                                                     |
| `deferred` | Explicitly out of scope for current roadmap                     |

## Done Rule

A workpackage is `done` when all of: (a) `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit` clean; (b) new logic has vitest / pytest unit coverage; (c) `make verify` passes; (d) merged to main and pushed.

input.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
chore(spec): consolidate spec/ into a single tracker; detangle PRD from runtime

Collapses 11 spec markdown files into one self-contained workpackage tracker
plus the architectural seed reference. The screenshot-driven PRD and
wave-by-wave specs were either describing already-shipped functionality or
duplicated content now live in the tracker. Outstanding backlog (~13 items
across exchange formats, CLI/agent loop, kernel/validation/plan views) is
now grouped in one place; locked decisions and OpenBIM stance moved inline.

Decouples the PRD from runtime/test code by moving the validation taxonomy
into prd_blocking_advisor_matrix.py rows (where it already lived as
prdNeedle field values) and dropping the "needles must appear verbatim in
PRD markdown" assertions. The advisor matrix API output
(prdAdvisorMatrix_v1) is unchanged.

- spec/: down to workpackage-master-tracker.md + target-house-seed.md
- v1_closeout_readiness_manifest: removed prd_file_present and
  tracker_workpackages_present gates (the latter was already silently
  broken since the old tracker was deleted)
- prd_tracker_reconciliation_v1 + its test: deleted (already 100% skipped
  after old tracker removal)
- ifc_stub.plannedEntitiesReference: now points at the tracker
- cli.mjs export error message: now points at the tracker

Tests: 89 PRD-related + 42 evidence/roundtrip tests pass after refactor.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
echo --- post-commit status ---
git log --oneline -3

message.content:
i want to do some more revit analysis to understand how far we've come, what further gaps there are. i recently watched a 7 hour video, where i roughly found these insights:
📚 Hyper-Granulare UX/UI-Analyse: Definitive BIM Guide (Gesamtdokument)
Ziel: Lückenlose Dokumentation der User Experience (UX) und des User Interfaces (UI) für die Ableitung von Software-Requirements, UX-Design-Patterns oder Schulungshandbüchern auf Basis des 7-stündigen "Definitive BIM Guide"-Workflows.

🕒 Stunde 1: Projekt-Setup, Ordnerstruktur & UI-Einführung
Zeitstempel
Aktion & UX/UI Beschreibung
00:00 - 02:29
Ressourcen & Datei-Management: Der Nutzer arbeitet außerhalb von Revit im Windows Explorer. Er demonstriert den Aufbau eines Common Data Environments (CDE). Er legt manuell Ordner an: Project Files, 00_Revision, 01_BIM Deliverables etc. UX-Relevanz: BIM beginnt mit starren Datenstrukturen.
07:43
Revit Interface & Projektstart: Im Home-Screen klickt der Nutzer unter "Models" auf New. UX-Workaround: Anstatt den "Browse"-Button zu nutzen, kopiert der Nutzer den Dateipfad der Template-Datei aus dem Windows Explorer und fügt den Pfad mit Ctrl+V ein, um sofort in den richtigen Ordner zu springen.
10:22
Projekt speichern & Backups: Klick auf File -> Save As -> Project. Wichtiges UI-Detail: Im Speichern-Dialog klickt er auf Options.... Dort ändert er "Maximum backups" von 20 (Standard) auf 1.
12:04
Worksets (Bearbeitungsbereiche): Klick im Ribbon auf Collaborate -> Worksets. Erstellung neuer Sets ("S-00_Grids and levels"). Revit fragt via Popup, ob das erstellte Workset aktiv werden soll. Er klickt No.
14:22
Ebenen (Levels) & Projekteinheiten: Doppelklick im Project Browser auf North. Einheiten-Anpassung: Shortcut UN. Im Format-Dialog ändert er die Einheiten auf "Meters" und "2 decimal places".
18:11
IFC und DWG verlinken: Reiter Insert -> Klick Link Revit. UX-Regel: Im Dialogfeld unten bei "Positioning" muss zwingend Origin to Origin ausgewählt werden.
20:19
Visibility/Graphics (Sichtbarkeit): Shortcut VV. Wechsel zum Reiter Revit Links -> Display Settings. Wechsel von "By Host View" zu By Linked View. Apply -> OK.
30:45
Stützen platzieren: Stütze ausgewählt. Klick auf Edit Type -> Duplicate. Name "20x45" eingeben. Kritischer UX-Schritt: In der Tabelle müssen die Werte "b" und "h" manuell auf 0.20 und 0.45 geändert werden. In der grünen Optionsleiste ändert er das Dropdown von Depth auf Height.
47:35
View Range (Ansichtsbereich): Ein generierter Träger ist nicht sichtbar. Klick ins Leere -> Properties Panel -> View Range -> Edit. Er ändert Cut Plane auf "3.92" und Bottom auf "3.8". Klick auf Apply. Der Träger taucht auf.
52:22
Split Element (SD): Er tippt SD. Der Cursor wird zu einem kleinen Messer. Er zerschneidet Träger in logische Einzelbauteile.


🕒 Stunde 2: Strukturwände, Modifikationen & Erste Decken
Zeitstempel
Aktion & UX/UI Beschreibung
01:00:16
Copy & Move Tipps: Element markieren, Shortcut CO (Copy) oder MV (Move). Setzen des Hakens bei Multiple in der Optionsleiste, um Befehle am Stück auszuführen, ohne das Tool neu zu starten.
01:11:11
Align Tool für Träger: Shortcut AL. Ein extrem wichtiges UI-Paradigma. Zuerst wird das Referenz-Objekt (die Ziel-Linie) angeklickt, dann das Objekt, das dorthin "springen" soll.
01:16:16
Structural Walls (Tragende Wände): Reiter Structure -> Wall. Die UI fordert eine Entscheidung in der Optionsleiste: Location Line.
01:18:35
Wall Centerline vs. Core Centerline: UX-Logik: "Wall Centerline" nimmt die geometrische Mitte der gesamten Wand inkl. Dämmung. "Core Centerline" nimmt die Mitte des tragenden Kerns (z.B. Beton). Der Nutzer wählt Finish Face: Exterior, um Wände sauber an der DWG-Fassadenlinie auszurichten.
01:26:18
Neue Wandtypen erstellen: Im Properties Panel Edit Type -> Duplicate. Unter "Structure" klickt er auf "Edit". Ein neues Tabellen-UI öffnet sich. Hier ändert er die Dicke (Thickness) der Tragschicht auf 0.25m.
01:39:09
Offsets & Anpassungen: Mehrere Wände werden markiert. Im Properties Panel wird der Base Offset (Abstand zum Boden) auf z.B. -0.1m gesetzt, damit die Betonwand tiefer als der fertige Fußboden auf der Rohdecke sitzt.
01:50:36
Decken (Slabs) ausrichten: Reiter Structure -> Floor. Der türkisene Skizziermodus startet. Nach dem Zeichnen mit "Pick Walls" nutzt er rigoros den Shortcut TR (Trim/Extend), um alle Ecken perfekt zu schließen, da das UI sonst Fehler ausgibt.


🕒 Stunde 3: Selektionswerkzeuge, Öffnungen & Deckenstrukturen
Zeitstempel
Aktion & UX/UI Beschreibung
02:00:06
Auswahl-Werkzeuge (Selection Tool): Auswahl von links nach rechts (durchgezogene Linie) = nur komplett eingeschlossene Elemente. Von rechts nach links (gestrichelte Linie) = alle berührten Elemente.
02:14:23
Schnitt-Ansicht (Section) für Öffnungen: Quick Access Toolbar -> Section. Schnittlinie ziehen, Doppelklick auf den blauen Pfeilkopf, um in die 2D-Schnittansicht der Wand zu wechseln.
02:17:16
Wall Opening erzeugen: Reiter Architecture -> Wall Opening. Wand anklicken, dann ein Rechteck aufziehen.
02:19:33
Formeln in Maßketten: Klick auf temporäre blaue Maßkette. Eingabe von =3.31-2.60. Revit berechnet den Wert und verschiebt die Kantenlinie millimetergenau.
02:27:16
Kopieren von Öffnungen: Wandöffnung markieren, Shortcut CO, Haken bei Multiple. Kopieren entlang der Achsen.
02:43:54
Tragende Decken skizzieren: Reiter Structure -> Floor. Klick auf Pick Walls in der Draw-Palette. Revit generiert pinke Begrenzungslinien auf der Innenseite der Wände. Offset auf -0.1 setzen.
02:52:13
Skizziermodus validieren: Klick auf grünen Haken. Gelbe Warnmeldung: "Highlighted floors overlap". Der Nutzer ignoriert dies zunächst und korrigiert die Geometrie manuell im Grundriss.


🕒 Stunde 4: Detaillierung, Planerstellung & Architektur-Start
Zeitstempel
Aktion & UX/UI Beschreibung
03:01:10
Wall Joins (Wandverbindungen): Reiter Modify -> Wall Joins Icon. Ecke anklicken. In der Optionsleiste auf Next klicken, bis die gewünschte Verschneidungsvariante (Gehrung, Stumpf) erreicht ist.
03:13:22
"Don't Attach" Dialog (UX-Kritik): Pop-up fragt: "Would you like walls that go up to this floor's level to attach to its bottom?" Der Nutzer klickt fast immer rigoros auf No, um unvorhersehbare Fehler bei manuellen Offsets zu vermeiden.
03:28:10
Starting View festlegen: Klick Manage -> Starting View. Auswahl eines neu erstellten Titel-Plans. Die Datei öffnet sich künftig immer mit diesem repräsentativen Plan.
03:30:42
Purge Unused: Klick Manage -> Purge Unused. OK klicken (zwingend 3 Mal wiederholen, um verschachtelte Objekte zu löschen). Dann Collaborate -> Synchronize with Central.
03:38:50
Copy/Monitor (Tragwerk überwachen): Architekturmodell gestartet. Reiter Collaborate -> Copy/Monitor -> Select Link. Tragwerk anklicken. Auf Copy klicken (Multiple). Grids markieren, Filter anwenden. Klick auf den kleinen Finish-Button, dann den großen Finish-Haken.
03:46:57
Architektur-DWG als Underlay: In der View drückt er VV. Deaktiviert IFCs. Stellt die Architektur-DWG unter "Display Settings" auf Underlay (Pauspapier-Effekt).


🕒 Stunde 5: Innenausbau, Filter & Curtain Walls
Zeitstempel
Aktion & UX/UI Beschreibung
04:02:55
https://github.com/jhoetter
04:08:34
Ceiling Plans (Decken): Reiter Architecture -> Ceiling -> Sketch Ceiling. Raumumrisse nachfahren. Fehler-Feedback "Line must be in closed loop" abarbeiten.
04:28:45
View Filters (Sichtbarkeitsfilter): Shortcut VV -> Filters -> Edit/New. Filter "Insulation" erstellen. Kategorie "Walls", Regel: Type Name contains ISO. Unter "Cut Pattern" eine Schraffur zuweisen. Wände erhalten vollautomatisch diese Schraffur.
04:36:49
Curtain Walls (Fassaden) & Türen: Reiter Architecture -> Wall -> Curtain Wall. Typ auf "Fixed Number" (z.B. 9) stellen. Tür einfügen: Maus über Glaspaneel bewegen, mehrfach TAB drücken, anklicken. Pin-Symbol anklicken (entsperren), dann in Properties gegen Tür tauschen.
04:53:01
Familien-Editor starten: File -> New -> Family -> Metric Profile Rail.rft. Weißes UI mit grünen Referenzebenen.
04:58:07
Parameter & EQ erzeugen: Maßband nutzen (Aligned Dimension). Über Mittelachse messen, kleines EQ-Symbol klicken (Symmetrie-Zwang). Gesamtbreite messen, im Ribbon auf Create Parameter klicken und "Glass Thickness" nennen.


🕒 Stunde 6: Geländer, Räume & Shared Parameters
Zeitstempel
Aktion & UX/UI Beschreibung
05:00:43
Geländer zusammenbauen: Familien ins Projekt geladen. Geländer markieren -> Edit Type -> Duplicate. Baluster Placement öffnen und eigene Familien (Trennstäbe) mit 2.00m Abstand zuweisen.
05:13:07
Worksets massenhaft zuweisen: Auswahlrechteck über Modell ziehen, Filter (nur Walls). Im Properties-Panel den Parameter Workset kollektiv auf "A-10_SOE" setzen.
05:27:33
Rooms platzieren (Raumbegrenzung): Reiter Architecture -> Room. UX-Problem: Wände des verlinkten Modells werden nicht erkannt. Strukturmodell markieren -> Properties -> Haken bei Room Bounding setzen.
05:35:37
Räume löschen (UX-Falle): Das Löschen des Stempels im Grundriss löscht nur die Repräsentation. Um die Datenbank zu bereinigen, muss ein Room Schedule erstellt und die Zeile via Delete Row gelöscht werden.
05:42:51
Shared Parameters: Reiter Manage -> Shared Parameters. Erstellung einer externen .txt-Datei. "Zone Program" als Text-Parameter anlegen. Danach via Project Parameters der Kategorie "Rooms" zuweisen.
05:57:40
Raumstempel Familien-Editor: Neuer Metric Room Tag. Um Text perfekt zu zentrieren, nutzt er den Workaround, ein Dummy-"I" zu tippen, Referenzlinien durch die Mitte zu ziehen, zu gruppieren und daran das Label auszurichten.


🕒 Stunde 7: Auswertung, Export & Navisworks Clash Detection
Zeitstempel
Aktion & UX/UI Beschreibung
06:13:03
Schedules formatieren: "Room Schedule" öffnen. Properties -> Sorting/Grouping. Nach "Zone Program" sortieren, Haken bei Header und Blank line setzen.
06:15:42
Flächen berechnen: Properties -> Formatting. Feld "Area" markieren und unten rechts im Dropdown von "No calculation" auf Calculate Totals umstellen.
06:21:03
Color Fill Legend: Reiter Architecture -> Room & Area -> Color Fill Legend. In Grundriss klicken. Space Type = "Rooms", Scheme = "Zone Program". Grundriss füllt sich automatisch mit Farben.
06:31:48
Ansichten (Crop Region): In den Ansichtseigenschaften Haken bei Crop Region Visible und Crop View setzen. Den schwarzen Rahmen eng ans Gebäude ziehen.
06:44:37
NWC-Export für Navisworks: 3D-Ansicht öffnen. File -> Export -> NWC. Kritisch: Unter Navisworks Settings den Haken bei "Convert linked files" setzen.
06:46:16
Navisworks Append: Navisworks Manage öffnen. Im Home-Reiter auf Append klicken. NWC-Dateien laden exakt übereinander.
06:54:24
Find Items & Selection Sets: Objekt anklicken -> Ribbon Select Same -> Select Same Type. Ribbon Save Selection klicken und z.B. als "MEP_Ducts" speichern.
07:01:20
Viewpoint-Workaround: App-Menü (blaues N) -> Options -> Interface -> Viewpoints Defaults. Haken bei Save Hide/Required Attributes setzen, sonst speichert das System keine ausgeblendeten Elemente im Viewpoint.
07:13:02
Clash Detective: Home-Reiter -> Clash Detective -> Add Test. Links Set "STR_Beams", rechts "MEP_Ducts" wählen. Toleranz einstellen. Klick auf Run Test. Die Kamera zoomt im "Results"-Tab millimetergenau auf die markierten Kollisionen (Rot/Grün).

content.text:
Here's the analysis. I'll cross-reference each Revit feature against actual code, marking ✅ shipped / 🟡 partial / ❌ missing — then highlight what's worth adding to the tracker.

## ✅ Already shipped (close-to-parity)

| Revit feature | bim-ai location |
|---|---|
| Worksets + mass workset assignment | `worksetId` field on elements; multi-select assignment (WP-V2-12) |
| Levels + units | `Level`/`level` element + project units |
| Place columns + duplicate type (b/h params) | `column` element (WP-V2-06) |
| **View Range** (cut plane, top/bottom) | `cutPlaneOffsetMm`, `viewRangeBottomMm/TopMm` on `plan_view` |
| Structural walls + Location Line (Wall Centerline / Core / Finish Face) | `WallLocationLine` field |
| Wall types with layered structure (Edit Type → Edit) | FL-08 (just shipped: `wallTypeCatalog.ts` + `meshBuilders.layeredWall.ts`) |
| Base Offset / Top Constraint | `baseConstraintLevelId/OffsetMm`, `topConstraintLevelId/OffsetMm` |
| Section views (double-click section head) | `section_cut` + SectionBox |
| Formula in dimensions (`=3.31-2.60`) | `evaluateExpression()` in Inspector |
| Starting View | `startingViewId` (WP-V2-12) |
| Purge Unused | `PurgeUnusedPanel` (WP-V2-12) |
| Copy/Monitor | `monitorSourceId` (WP-V2-12) |
| Underlay (level-based) | `underlayLevelId` on `plan_view` |
| Ceiling Plans | `ceiling` element (WP-V2-08) |
| **View Filters (rule-based graphics overrides)** | `viewFilters` array + `VVDialog.tsx` |
| Curtain Walls + door insertion via TAB cycle | `isCurtainWall` + `tab-cycle-location` event in tool grammar |
| Family Editor (parametric, EQ, type params) | WP-V2-11 |
| Railings + baluster geometry | shipped |
| Rooms + Room Bounding on linked elements | WP-V2-09 |
| Room delete via Schedule (UX gotcha) | WP-V2-09 |
| Shared Parameters + Project Parameters | WP-V2-10 |
| Schedule Sorting/Grouping (header + blank line) | WP-V2-10 |
| Calculate Totals on field | WP-V2-10 |
| Color Fill Legend | WP-V2-09 |
| Find Items / Select Same Type | WP-V2-13 |
| Save Selection (Selection Sets) | WP-V2-13 |
| Viewpoints with hidden/isolated visibility | WP-V2-13 |
| Clash Detection | WP-V2-13 |

That's **27 of ~40 Revit workflow features** essentially at parity.

## 🟡 Partial (data model exists, plan-canvas tool is a stub)

This is the biggest finding — **9 plan-canvas authoring tools are stubs** in `packages/web/src/plan/PlanCanvas.tsx`:

| Stub | Line | Effect |
|---|---|---|
| `splitWall` | 1103 | Tool registered (SD shortcut), prints `console.warn` instead of splitting |
| `alignElement` | 1085 | Tool registered (AL shortcut), no-op |
| `trimElement` | 1158 | TR shortcut, no-op |
| `wall-opening` | 924 | Wall Opening tool (Hour 3) — no-op |
| `wall-join` | 1409 | Wall Joins (mitre/butt switching) — no-op |
| `column placement` | 1256 | API can create columns; canvas tool is stubbed |
| `beam placement` | 1265 | Same |
| `ceiling` | 1286 | Same |
| `shaft` | 1243 | Slab opening shaft tool stubbed |

**Net effect:** the model can hold all these element kinds (you can author via CLI/API/agent), but the plan-canvas drawing tools that the Revit user actually clicks on are no-ops. For a human authoring workflow this is a big gap; for an agent-authoring workflow (which is BIM AI's actual emphasis) it's less critical.

## ❌ Missing entirely

| Revit feature | Status | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| **Linked external models** (IFC / DWG / Revit) | No `kind: 'link'` element | No federation concept; Copy/Monitor only works intra-model |
| **Underlay from linked DWG/IFC** (tracing-paper effect) | — | `underlayLevelId` is intra-model only |
| **Synchronize with Central / Worksharing** | — | bim-ai is server-authoritative single-model; no central-file split-up |
| **Floor sketching with Pick Walls + Trim/Extend (TR) loop** | Partial | `floor` element exists with `boundaryMm` but Pick Walls UX + auto-trim isn't there |
| **Wall-to-floor "Don't Attach" dialog** | — | No wall-attach concept |
| **Wall Joins (interactive mitre/butt switching)** | Partial | `wall_join_evidence.py` analyses joins; no UI to switch the variant |
| **Wall Opening as a distinct tool** | Stub | Currently can only model openings as door/window/slab_opening |
| **Crop Region UI toggle on plan canvas** | Partial | `cropMinMm/MaxMm` data exists; only used on sheet viewports, not interactive plan crop |
| **Floor overlap warning** | — | No floor-overlap validation rule |
| **Sketch-mode "Line must be in closed loop" feedback** | — | No sketch validator |
| **Project Units dialog (UN shortcut)** | Partial | Units exist in code but no Manage → Project Units dialog |
| **Room Tag family in Family Editor** | Partial | Tags exist but no tag-family authoring |
| **NWC export** | — | No NWC writer; gltf/ifc only |
| **Federated Navisworks-style coordination** | — | Clash detection works on host model only, can't append external NWCs |

## Recommendation: which gaps to add to the tracker

Most of these aren't worth tracking (they're either UX-niceties or workflow patterns we deliberately diverge from — e.g. central file / worksharing doesn't fit our server-authoritative model). But these **6 are worth backlog rows**:

1. **`UI-01` — Plan-canvas authoring tools de-stub** (split / align / trim / wall-opening / wall-join / column / beam / ceiling / shaft — 9 tools sharing the same pattern). Big quality-of-life for human authoring.
2. **`KRN-04` — `wall_opening` element kind** (rectangular openings independent of door/window family — common in basements / shafts / mechanical chases).
3. **`VAL-02` — Floor / slab overlap detection** (mirrors Revit's "Highlighted floors overlap" warning).
4. **`KRN-05` — Linked external models** (`link_revit` / `link_ifc` / `link_dwg` element kinds + federation for clash + underlay). Substantial work.
5. **`EXC-01` — NWC export** (small, mainly a writer for `app/bim_ai/export_nwc.py`).
6. **`PLN-02` — Interactive plan crop region** (toggle visibility + drag handles for `cropMinMm/MaxMm` on `plan_view`).

The big strategic one is `KRN-05` (linked models / federation). Without it, BIM AI is a single-model authoring environment, not a coordination environment — Navisworks-style clash across multiple discipline models won't ever be possible. That's a real product-shape decision.

Want me to add the 6 rows to the tracker, or just discuss further first?

content.text:
This is a good strategic moment to frame the gap properly. The 14-item list I gave you is misleading — it's really **3 architectural primitives** that, once built, unlock most of what's missing. Let me lay them out as I see them and tie them back to your two specific asks.

## Reframing the gap: 3 missing primitives

### Primitive 1 — Federation as a first-class element kind

This is the unblocker for your "Navisworks-level coordination without file round-trip" ask. Today bim-ai is a single-model authoring environment. Revit's federation works because every linked file has stable element IDs that survive across reload. **You actually have a structural advantage here**: bim-ai is database-backed, so linked elements never need round-tripping through NWC/IFC just to give them stable IDs.

Concrete shape:

```ts
{
  kind: 'link_model',
  id: '...',
  sourceModelId: '<bim-ai model UUID>',  // another model in the same DB
  positionMm: { xMm, yMm, zMm },
  rotationDeg: 0,
  originAlignmentMode: 'origin_to_origin' | 'project_origin' | 'shared_coords',
  visibility: 'host_view' | 'linked_view',
  worksetId?: string,
}
```

The host model treats the link's elements as read-only renderable context. Selection works, snap-to works, clash queries work. Edits are blocked at the engine level. Copy/Monitor already 80% works for this — `monitorSourceId` becomes cross-model.

This is the killer primitive. With it:
- **Clash detection across disciplines** (Architecture host + linked Structure + linked MEP, all in-DB, no NWC export). Today's WP-V2-13 clash test trivially extends to cross-link sets.
- **Copy/Monitor across disciplines** (architect monitors structural grid changes).
- **Eventually: Revit/IFC/DWG import** writes a "shadow" bim-ai model and creates a `link_model` row pointing at it. The user explicitly mentioned this is OK as long as collaboration stays DB-native.
- **Worksets become per-link**: assign which discipline each link belongs to.

It also means `Synchronize with Central` is **already obsolete** in your architecture — you don't need it because every command is server-authoritative on commit. That's a genuine product advantage worth marketing.

### Primitive 2 — In-place editing (canvas grips + 3D handles)

This is your "edit directly from the 3D view" ask. Today the Inspector panel is the primary editing surface; in Revit it's the secondary one — primary is on-canvas grips with live temporary dimensions.

What's actually needed:

| Concept | What ships | What's missing |
|---|---|---|
| Selection grips | Inspector panel binds to selection | No on-canvas grip handles for endpoints / midpoints / heights |
| Temporary dimensions | — | Auto-generated blue dimensions to nearest neighbours when something is selected; click → editable; lock icon constrains |
| Drag-to-edit on plan | — | Drag wall endpoint → live preview → commit `MoveWallEndpoints`; numeric override during drag |
| Drag-to-edit on 3D | — | Click wall top edge → vertical handle → drag to change height (commits `topConstraintOffsetMm`) |
| Constraint locks (padlock) | Constraint exists in data | No UI to lock-via-temp-dimension on canvas |

This is the **single highest-ROI change** to the editing experience. It's not a model-kernel change — it's a viewport-interaction change. The data model already supports everything (constraint-driven heights, location-line offsets, host relationships); the UX just doesn't expose it.

The 9 plan-canvas stubs (split / align / trim / wall-opening / wall-join / column / beam / ceiling / shaft) all share this same missing infrastructure: **no canonical "tool emits a preview, user confirms or cancels, command commits"** lifecycle wired into PlanCanvas. Once you build that for one tool properly, the others fall into place quickly.

### Primitive 3 — Sketch-mode authoring (closed-loop sessions)

In Revit, ~half the model-authoring tools (floor, ceiling, roof, room separation, in-place mass, void cut, sweep path) work by entering a **modal sketch session**: you draw a closed loop, the sketch is validated (closed, planar, non-self-intersecting), then Finish commits. Cancel discards.

bim-ai today supports the *output* of sketch sessions (floors with `boundaryMm`, ceilings, openings) but not the **session itself**:
- No "enter sketch mode" → "draw lines / pick walls" → "Finish ✓ / Cancel ✗" flow on canvas
- No sketch-validation feedback ("Line must be in closed loop", "Highlighted floors overlap")
- No "Pick Walls" sketch mode (auto-derive boundary from selected walls)

This is what unlocks irregular geometry. Today, an agent can author irregular floors via API but a human can't draw an L-shaped floor on canvas.

---

## How these primitives map onto the 14-item gap list

| Tier | Primitive | Unlocks |
|---|---|---|
| **1** | Federation (P1) | Cross-discipline clash, Copy/Monitor across links, future Revit/IFC/DWG import via shadow models, multi-discipline workspace, NWC obsolescence |
| **1** | In-place editing (P2) | All 9 plan-canvas tool stubs, 3D direct manipulation, "edit anything from the 3D view", on-canvas dimensions, constraint locks |
| **2** | Sketch mode (P3) | Floor pick-walls, ceiling pick-walls, roof footprint editing, hip / L-shape roof support (KRN-02/03), wall opening tool, in-place generic mass, room separation |
| 3 | Snapping upgrade | Better authoring feel — but not load-bearing |
| 3 | Detail components / 2D linework | Documentation polish |
| 3 | NWC export | Probably skip given P1 makes it unnecessary |

## My recommendation

**Sequence: P1 → P2 → P3.**

- P1 (federation) is independently scoped, has clear data-model shape, and unlocks the user's stated Navisworks ask in one step. Estimated 2–3 weeks. The clash extension on top is small.
- P2 (in-place editing) is the highest-ROI UX change but its surface area is large — every tool needs to participate in the grip + temp-dimension protocol. Pick **one element kind first** (walls), build the full grip/temp-dimension/drag/numeric-override flow end-to-end, then propagate. Estimated 3–4 weeks for walls alone done well, then ~1 week per additional element kind.
- P3 (sketch mode) is the biggest engineering lift and benefits less from agent-authoring (agents author via API; sketch-mode is a human UX). Worth doing but lower priority unless the human-authoring use case becomes central.

What I would **not** recommend:
- Adding 14 separate backlog rows. They're symptoms.
- Pursuing Revit `.rvt` import directly. With P1 you can ingest IFC/DWG into a shadow bim-ai model and link it; no proprietary RVT parser needed. RVT plugin is a much later "for customers who insist" item.

## Open question for you

I see two reasonable next steps:

1. **Draft a design doc for P1 (federation)** — element-kind schema, engine-side read-only enforcement, clash extension, viewport rendering of linked elements. ~1 page; gives a concrete plan. *Recommended if you want to build it.*

2. **Replace the current backlog tables with a 3-primitive roadmap** — restructure `workpackage-master-tracker.md` so it reads as P1/P2/P3 with the existing 13 backlog items folded under them as sub-tasks. *Recommended if you want the tracker itself to reflect the strategy.*

Which feels more useful right now? Or is there a third framing you'd prefer — e.g. focusing strategically on P2 (3D direct editing) since that's the most visceral gap when watching the Revit videos?
